title.home: Welcome to Ballfish's Little Fish Tank

subtitle.home: Let's explore what's in this fish tank

about.name: Name: Tzu-Yin Hong
about.dream: Dream: To become a full-stack engineer.
about.charactor: Introverted and shy by nature, but enjoys being around people. Tends to look for answers independently when facing problems, loves sharing progress when projects hit new milestones, and prefers thinking about product design over simply finishing projects.
about.interest: Wide range of interests: currently enjoys volunteering at open source community conferences, coding, jazz drumming, improv show, and writing novels. Previously loved dragon boat racing.

cloud.title: Cloud Platforms
cloud.China: Tencent Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, Huawei Cloud

experienceKey.COSCUP: Conference for Open Source Coders, Users, and Promoters

experience.university: Graduated from Soochow University, Department of Information Management. During freshman year, worked part-time at the Computer Center, mainly responsible for BBS management and server room maintenance. During junior year, participated in a college student research project, creating a game using Unity3D to teach children programming, winning the Outstanding Creative Award for the college student project. During junior year, interned at Hengding Technology for one year, mainly writing web crawler frameworks in C#. During senior year (second semester), independently sought employment and joined Wavenet Technology for frontend development until graduation.
experience.Wavenet: 2018/01~2019/02 In 2018 (senior year, second semester), independently sought employment. Responsible for frontend development of backend reports and some backend development. Frontend: React, AngularJS (v1); Backend: NodeJS EggJS framework. Had practical experience with Docker and Docker Compose at that time. Reason for leaving: Wanted to explore other industries.
experience.Universetech: 2019/04~now Joined Universetech International in April 2019 as a frontend engineer. Used React to develop backend management interfaces and logic, building the entire backend frontend architecture from scratch. In 2020, helped fix bugs in the customer service system backend written in Golang. From 2021, assisted with main backend system development using PHP Laravel. Started researching ClickHouse in 2021, establishing ClickHouse infrastructure including deployment on Kubernetes and backup mechanisms. In 2022, refactored the customer service system frontend and backend architecture, improved the frontend structure, and migrated the backend from Golang to Laravel. Also began assisting the SRE team: setting up GitLab Runner auto-scaling on Kubernetes, deploying Vault Server, and establishing emergency maintenance mechanisms. In 2023, assisted with ArgoCD and Vault integration, backend config centralization architecture planning and implementation, main system production-like sandbox environment planning and implementation, and data analytics team system architecture planning and implementation. In 2024, assisted the data analytics team in training customer behavior analysis models. Helped the product routing team plan routing systems. Assisted with communication software infrastructure and developed parts of the app (Flutter) and backend (Golang, Laravel). In 2025, took on the CEO role, promoted cross-team book clubs and project retrospective activities, assisted with communication software project refactoring and migration, and handled gomobile, Java AAR package publishing, Swift Package management, and Flutter Plugin management. Assisted with product routing optimization and in-app routing switching development, mainly working with Capacitor, Swift, and Kotlin. In 2026, stepped down as CEO and took on the SRE team lead role, continuing to assist with product routing optimization, improving company security awareness, and helping refactor push notification features for the communication software.
experience.SITCON: First encountered SITCON (Students' Information Technology Conference) in 2015, joining the tech community. In 2016, served as SITCON 2017 Administrative Team Leader. In 2017, served as SITCON 2018 Vice General Coordinator, helping organize that year's conference.
experience.MOPCON: First encountered MOPCON (Mobile Oriented Developers Conference) in 2016, serving as an MOPCON 2016 Administrative Team Member. In 2025, served as MOPCON 2025 PR Team Member, responsible for writing promotional content.
experience.Rust: Organized a Rust study group in the Rust community in 2018.
experience.COSCUP: Joined COSCUP (Conference for Open Source Coders, Users, and Promoters) in 2019, mainly responsible for conference program affairs. Continued handling program affairs in 2020. In 2021, served as Intern General Coordinator, learning how COSCUP events are organized. In 2022–2023, served as General Coordinator, helping organize the respective conferences. In 2024–2025, served as Marketing Team Member, responsible for writing promotional content. Also served as Finance Team Member in 2025, responsible for accounting and recording that year's financial details. In 2026, served as Finance Team Leader, assisting with financial operations.
experience.g0v: Started engaging with g0v (Zero Government) Summit Biennial Conference in 2025. Served as Administrative Team Leader for g0v Summit 2026, responsible for conference administrative affairs.
experience.else:
Personal life and hobbies:
Beyond the typical ACG (Animation, Comics, Games) interests.
From 2019 to 2023, participated in an amateur dragon boat team, competing in: New Taipei City Dragon Boat President's Cup, Taipei City International Dragon Boat Racing Championship, Hualien International Dragon Boat Festival, Kaohsiung City Cup, and other races.
In 2024, participated in a working holiday in Japan, experiencing Japanese culture for about a year.
From 2025 to 2026, started exploring improv theater and learning performing arts.

supercube.title: Super Cube
supercube.description: A game created during university as a college research project, aimed at teaching children programming. Currently for display only, as it may no longer run on modern computers.

localMemoCli.title: Memo Rust
localMemoCli.description: Young and naive, thought JavaScript could rule the world, then discovered NodeJS startup speed was unbearably slow, so rewrote the memo tool in Rust.

bokoyaki.title: Bokoyaki
bokoyaki.description: A super simple Bokoyaki game made on a whim, and a tribute to the flash Takoyaki game from the old days.
